{{Quote|I truly hope your friend is all right, Han! ...But you were just taking me for a ''short joy-ride, remember-''??|Gyla Petro|<ref name="The Constancia Affair" />}}
'''Gyla Petro''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from [[Kalgo 13]]. An [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]]<ref name="The Kashyyyk Depths" /> and a member of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region's [[House Petro]],<ref name="Endnotes">{{Blog|danwallace|147|Endnotes for Star Wars: The Essential Atlas (part 5 of 5)}}</ref> she was the only surviving member of a research party massacred by plants on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].<ref name="The Kashyyyk Depths" /> She later joined [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] for a journey to [[Constancia/Legends|Constancia]].<ref name="The Constancia Affair" />

==Biography==
Gyla Petro was a born into the aristocratic family [[House Petro]]. At some point she became a member of [[New Worlds]] as a biologist. Some of these biologists along with some Imperial soldiers traveled to the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] in order to obtain some roots of the [[Orga/Legends|Orga]] plant. When the soldiers destroyed plants to make a pathway through the forest, the plants got upset and started killing the Imperials. Gyla was the only survivor due to the intervention of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. After being saved, Gyla was taken to a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] city on the back of a [[Sureggi/Legends|Sureggi]] and participated in the celebration of [[Life Day/Legends|Life Day]]. However, shortly afterwards, Solo ended up captured by Imperials and forced to descend to the lowest and foulest level of Kashyyyk, which led to suspicion of Petro being an Imperial spy.

Sometime later, Gyla was traveling aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] with Han Solo and Chewbacca. Han received as SOS message from [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and found the droids [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] in the wreckage of Luke's ship. Upset at the detour, Gyla noted that she didn't ask to join a mission of mercy. C-3PO explained Luke's mission to Han, further upsetting Gyla. She thought Han was just a smuggler and not a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Gyla wanted to leave when Han piloted the ship away from Imperial forces, though she had no choice but to come along for the ride. As the ''Millennium Falcon'' escaped Imperial forces by going through [[Constancia/Legends|Constancia's]] ring, Gyla continued to beg to be let go. Han escaped from the Imperials and piloted the ship into a hidden base inside the ring. Once landed, Han got off to investigate while Gyla stayed aboard. Luke, [[Gamine]] and [[Sharlee]] were at this base and once it was deemed safe, Gyla came off the ship. Everyone devised a strategy to get Gamine to the surface of the planet. Han would go with Sharlee and hijack a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] while Gyla, Luke, Gamine, Chewbacca, R2-D2 and C-3PO would fly towards the surface in the ''Millennium Falcon''. The distraction worked, and once the ''Falcon'' made it to the surface, they were all celebrated as heroes.

Petro's suspected status of espionage for the Empire was later included in [[Gyla Petro (file)|her files]] on the planet [[Fusai/Legends|Fusai]], specifically on the Rebel-owned [[BRT supercomputer/Legends|BRT supercomputer]] [[Mistress Mnemos]], although Mnemos was unaware of the exact details behind why she was suspected of being a spy.
